name: Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop
name_original: Embalse de Baños desde Hervás – circular por Puerto de Béjar y Baños de Montemayor
description: Cycle the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop, a difficult 17.0-mile route through oak forests and past historic railway sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hervás are situated within the Ambroz Valley, characterized by its varied terrain and natural features. The region includes extensive forests, such as the prominent Chestnut Forest, and is traversed by rivers and waterfalls. Cyclists encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with routes featuring significant elevation changes and mountain passes. This landscape offers diverse conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Hervás The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows sections of the Vía de la Plata Greenway and passes by the Embalse de Baños. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Hervás – Jewish Quarter of Hervás loop from Hervás, a difficult 45.6 miles (73.3 km) path. This route offers expansive views of Hervás and explores the historic Jewish Quarter. Local touring cyclists also love the La Cantina de la Estación – View of Hervás loop from Hervás, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of Hervás, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Hervás is defined by the Ambroz Valley, chestnut forests, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Hervás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hervás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of touring cycling routes can I expect around Hervás?
A: Hervás, situated in the Ambroz Valley, offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 150 options available. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, ranging from relaxed rides to challenging climbs with significant elevation changes and loose stones. The landscape includes extensive forests, rivers, and mountain passes, providing diverse conditions for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in Hervás feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for varying skill levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantina de la Estación – View of Hervás loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.4-mile (15.1 km) trail that offers varied terrain and views of Hervás, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Hervás area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Hervás offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hervás – Jewish Quarter of Hervás loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 45.6-mile (73.3 km) path with over 1300 meters of elevation gain, exploring the historic Jewish Quarter. The region also features longer routes like the 'nor3xtrem cicloturista' (161.8 km, 2,929 m elevation) and 'Pto. Honduras-Piornal-Pto. Honduras' (168 km, 3,018 m elevation) for those looking for multi-day adventures.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Hervás?
A: The most popular times for touring cycling in Hervás are typically March and April. During these months, the weather is generally pleasant, making for ideal conditions to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Hervás?
A: Yes, many routes in the Hervás region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120707969/via-verde-de-la-plata-embalse-de-banos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 17.0-mile (27.4 km) circular trail that follows sections of the Vía de la Plata Greenway and passes by the Baños Reservoir.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Ambroz Valley is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter features like the extensive Chestnut Forest, beautiful rivers, and waterfalls such as the Chorro de la Meancera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are also a notable highlight in the area. Many routes offer views of the valley and reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Hervás is famous for its well-preserved Jewish Quarter, with its narrow, cobblestone streets and unique architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hervás – Jewish Quarter of Hervás loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; specifically incorporates this historic area. You might also pass by the 13th-century Church of Santa María or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4267031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos V Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Hervás?
A: The touring cycling routes around Hervás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Ambroz Valley, and the historical charm of the region.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, a significant draw for touring cyclists is the Vía de la Plata Greenway (Vía Verde de la Plata). This route utilizes old railway lines converted into cycling paths, offering a pleasant experience with fantastic views of the Ambroz Valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120707969/via-verde-de-la-plata-embalse-de-banos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop&lt;/a&gt; incorporates sections of this greenway.

Q: Can I find viewpoints along the touring cycling trails in Hervás?
A: Many routes in Hervás offer expansive views of the Ambroz Valley and surrounding landscapes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas del Monte Rest Area – La Cantina de la Estación loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous Chestnut Forest?
A: The region is known for its prominent Chestnut Forest, and many routes traverse this area, especially in autumn when the foliage is vibrant. While no specific route listed here is named after it, exploring the trails within the Ambroz Valley will likely lead you through parts of this beautiful forest.
tips: It is worth deviating from the N-630 just to visit Hervás and its historic quarter, which houses one of the best-preserved Jewish quarters in Spain along with other stately monumental structures.
Comfortable route with spectacular views and surroundings at any time of the year
Incredible views from the Vía Verde de la Plata cycling route with views of the valley and the Baños Reservoir.
A beautiful tunnel on the route of the Vía Verde de la Plata, one kilometer long more or less. The lighting is automatic.

Very close to the Béjar Greenway, ideal for cyclists and hikers who follow the old railway line.
Cuisine: Mediterranean and Spanish, with grilled meats, tapas, and traditional dishes.
Specialties:

Grilled meats (T-bone steak, Iberian pork shoulder).
Burgers (including Wagyu).
A variety of tapas, croquettes, and salads.
Fresh fish and homemade stews.
Along the amazing Vía Verde cycle route is this stone mile of the old Jacobean route.

You can enjoy a pleasant walk around the reservoir and visit its dam. On the hottest days you can take a dip, but keep in mind that the shore can be a bit muddy.

Nearby there is a campsite with basic facilities where you can rest and enjoy the starry nights away from the light pollution of the city.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows a disused railway line, offering consistently gentle gradients. The surface is generally firm, compacted earth, making it well-suited for touring bicycles. You'll experience mostly flat sections with very slight inclines and declines.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its gentle gradients and well-maintained surface, this route is considered easy and suitable for cyclists of all skill levels, including beginners and families. It's a comfortable ride with manageable elevation changes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop is approximately 27.4 km (17 miles) long. On average, it takes about 2 hours to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I see along the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest, including the historic Jewish Quarter of Hervás, and the Tunnel on the Vía Verde de la Plata, which is illuminated by motion sensors. You'll also pass by the Baños Reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several scenic viewpoints. You can enjoy a View of Hervás and a beautiful View of Embalse de Baños. The trail also provides panoramic vistas of the Ambroz Valley and the Sierra de Béjar mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in towns like Hervás or Baños de Montemayor, which are connected by the Vía Verde de la Plata and serve as good starting points for the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, Vías Verdes in Spain are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling environments and to carry water for them, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter cycling is possible, but check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Vía Verde de la Plata or the Embalse de Baños Loop. The route traverses the Sierra de Candelario Natural Area, but access to the Vía Verde itself is free.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects towns like Hervás and Baños de Montemayor, where you can find various services. You'll pass by places like La Pequeña Estación Bar Restaurant Café, which is a restored station offering refreshments. These towns also provide opportunities for meals and breaks.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Plata - Embalse de Baños Loop incorporates significant sections of the official Vía Verde de la Plata (Casas del Monte - Béjar) and the Mtb Vía de la Plata. It also briefly touches upon sections of the Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca.
